title: Central processing unit

infobox.value: (**CPU**), also known as a **central processor**, **main processor**, or simply **processor**, is the primary [processor](/wiki/Processor_(computing) "Processor (computing)") in a given [computer](/wiki/Computer "Computer").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Its [electronic circuitry](/wiki/Electronic_circuit "Electronic circuit") executes [instructions](/wiki/Instruction_(computing) "Instruction (computing)") of a [computer program](/wiki/Computer_program "Computer program"), such as [arithmetic](/wiki/Arithmetic "Arithmetic"), logic, controlling, and [input/output](/wiki/Input/output "Input/output") (I/O) operations.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) This role contrasts with that of external components, such as [main memory](/wiki/Main_memory "Main memory") and I/O circuitry,[[6]](#cite_note-kuck-6) and specialized [coprocessors](/wiki/Coprocessor "Coprocessor") such as [graphics processing units](/wiki/Graphics_processing_unit "Graphics processing unit") (GPUs).
